what_it_does: This file is a structured interpretive extraction of a 17-slide investor pitch deck for ConceptV, documenting its narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual system, layout rules, and a catalog of design findings with recipes and generator rules.
observations: The deck is described as 17 slides across problem, solution, product, traction, commercialisation, and raise sections.
It records a detailed attention-flow path for every slide (p-01 through p-17).
It classifies findings into categories such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, and sequence.
Each finding includes a name, recipe, evidence, an emergent boolean, and a generator_rule.
Specific design tokens are supplied, including warm cream ground (#F8F4EE), primary gold (#C8960A), deep gold (#8B6914), and charcoal text (#1A1A1A / #333333 / #555555).
Shape semantics are strict: hexagon for brand-core nodes, diamond for Vi identity, circles for people/concept nodes, rounded rectangles for tags/buttons/timeline nodes.
A persistent V-circle badge is required on every non-photo slide bottom-right.
A diagonal gold-and-white stripe bar is required at the bottom edge of every non-photo slide.
The file ends with a truncation note, cutting off the sequence section mid-rule.
standouts: The deck is treated as a generative rule system rather than a static visual description, with explicit generator_rule statements for nearly every pattern.
Screenshot presentation technique is used as an argument: chaotic overlapping screenshots mean fragmentation, while straight shadow-framed screenshots mean order.
A deliberate chrome-mode contrast exists between the light, premium document deck and the dark, software-like product UI screenshots.
The diagonal stripe footer and persistent V-badge create a strong slide-boundary and brand-anchor system across the body slides.
conventions: Follows a JSON object structure with collection, depth_level, what_it_is, narrative_arc, attention_flow, and findings.
Findings use a consistent schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule.
Uses plain-language but precise design terminology (e.g., full-bleed, near-white/warm-cream, computed equal inset).
Color values are given as approximate hex codes and rgba tuples.
Layout proportions are expressed as percentages of slide width/height.
Observations are written as neutral, file-local facts without ranking or comparison.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end of the sequence section, so the final generator rule is incomplete.
Many measurements are approximate ('approx', 'appears to be', '~') and lack a stated derivation method or source resolution.
There is no embedded image data or checksum, so the descriptions cannot be directly verified against the original deck.
Some findings mix factual description with inferred intent (e.g., 'this is NOT a decorative accident').
notes: A dense, rule-based design-language ledger for a ConceptV pitch deck, useful as a generative brief but truncated near the end.
questions: What is the original file format and resolution of the source pitch deck?
How were the percentages, pixel equivalents, and color values measured or sampled?
Who produced this extraction and what validation process was used?
What does the ConceptV product actually do beyond the visual annotations shown?
Is the truncation at the end accidental or was the source material itself incomplete?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to document the pitch deck's visual DNA and generator rules; the file does exactly that, though it is cut off before finishing the sequence section.
apparent_themes: Visual grammar as a reproducible system
Consistency through tokens and persistent atoms
Problem/solution contrast encoded in visual treatment
Premium document aesthetic over screen-native UI
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to reverse-engineer the pitch deck into a machine-readable design system so the same visual language can be regenerated, fused, or audited programmatically.
novelty: It treats a pitch deck as a code/design substrate with explicit attention-flow choreography, shape semantics, and generator rules rather than as a mere collection of slides.
contribution: It contributes a detailed, tokenized specification of the deck's visual language, including color tokens, layout proportions, shape semantics, per-slide attention paths, and generator constraints that a fused system could use to reproduce or check consistency.
summary_for_embedding: A structured design extraction of a 17-slide ConceptV investor pitch deck, capturing narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual tokens, layout rules, and generator-rule findings across categories like geometry, color, typography, texture, and chrome mode.
intention_for_embedding: To encode the pitch deck's visual DNA as a reproducible design system and generative brief for downstream synthesis or consistency checking.
